item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's dark, it's disgusting, involves cannibalism and disturbing religious iconography.

I love it.

+1
100 net upvotes with 50 on both articles. Big hype

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Last sentence in the first paragraph of the description threw me off a little bit.

Upon discovery, SCP-4756 was a mummified seven month old fetus, but SCP-4756 has since developed into multiple entities over the course of thirteen years.

Maybe it's just me, but as written, it kind of reads like the fetus became multiple different entities, as in, it split apart and now there's many entities as opposed to a single entity changing forms.

+ Here's my two cents
- UNSOLICITED!

Upon discovery, SCP-4756 was a mummified seven month old fetus, but has since transitioned through multiple forms over the course of thirteen years.

Overall v. spooky. I appreciate it lots.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
multiple autopsies have shown that SCP-4756 only has two fully functioning organs, the heart and the brain, other organs are either non-functioning, heavily damaged, or missing.

Grammar issue here due to two full phrases being separated by a comma. I understand why this is desired to be done, since the sentence is also connected to a semicolon. A period is still okay even if it 'feels' awkward. I would rather these related full phrases be sentences in their own paragraph if the author really wants to cement their cohesion as an idea.

+ show block
– hide block

Upon discovery, SCP-4756 was a mummified seven month old fetus, but SCP-4756 has since developed into multiple entities over the course of thirteen years.

I would prefer this be the first sentence. It makes much more sense logically to have this as the opening sentence, then have it followed by "currently….".

Addendum 4756-2:

If this addendum tab needs to wrap, the wrapped tabs stretch on the second line, looking weird. Can you check if this is the behavior of Sigma-9 also?

There was something about that meat that every fiber of my being rejected, hours after I was still vomiting.

Same comma issue here. Which is weird because the rest of the journal is written with good grammar, so it's clearly not like a child is writing this.

It all happened so fast, I felt something primal awaken inside of me

Again here.

I saw him there, his skin was a dark grey.

Again.

I took him to the ritual site, I had to prepare him before the ritual can begin.

Is this intentional?


Long post collapsed. ~Zyn

Overall it is short, the image is compelling, but the prose is somewhat loose and the implementation is a little clumsy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a bit more than just cannibalizing a dead fetus, but what that is I'll leave for everyone to figure out on their own.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Consider me well and spooked. +1

The link to 4758 is interesting; while this one isn't quite fully parse-able without having read it, it still works; it's extremely unnerving and jarring and only increases in horror after you have the context.

Some very minor things:

+ show block
– hide block

It all happened so fast, I felt something primal awaken inside of me.

I saw the look of desperation in his eyes, he was praying to every god he knew for me to put him out of his misery.

Take this with a grain of salt because it's malleable in creative work like this, especially in personal letter-writing, but: in the above examples, you have some comma splices where a separate sentence or semicolon would be correct. It makes the pacing a little weird, at least to someone over-attuned to grammar like I am.

I took him and wrapped him in what was left of his mother's dress. I took him to the ritual site, I had to prepare him before the ritual can begin.

This is technically grammatically correct, but I'm not sure the tense it's in now is intentional; either way the verb switching in the last sentence is jarring (plus the comma splice). Consider: "I took him and wrapped him in what was left of his mother's dress. I took him to the ritual site. He must be prepared before the ritual can begin."

Non-grammatical: I have mixed feelings about the frequency of INFOHAZARD REDACTED, not so much because I don't buy the explanation you gave to aismallard above but because I'm not quite sure their volume here corresponds with the context I can read. Like, in entry 7, the six line-broken repetitions between "…fulfill my hunger." and "I didn't stop" are weird to me because I'm not sure it's reasonable that there would be seven paragraphs' worth of information there. The gap maybe isn't big enough to justify the blankness, if that makes sense.

But, on the other hand, the density of them right there does very much nail an alarm-bell "this is bad, it's really bad, oh no" into the reader's brain. If you're confident and comfortable that they're a reasonable-enough representation of how much would be there absent the infohazard, I wouldn't change it. (and, also: ew, seven paragraphs of that scene)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Spoilers for two articles
 

So, the reason why there are so many [INFOHAZARD REDACTED]s is because as the ritual draws closer Charles becomes more and more unstable. I was thinking having more pop up as the journals go on would be good enough since as seen in 4758, the letters become much more powerful and dangerous a time goes on. So with that in mind it might make the [INFOHAZARD REDACTED]s a bit better.


But, your opinion is your opinion. If you feel the same way with this explanation, oh well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how much is being unsaid here, however I think the transition in the journal entries could be more elegant, and the extensive [INFOHAZARD REDACTED]s got a bit tiring. I would try to reduce it to 1-3 per tab.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a new parent I had to close this article after finishing it and take a good two hours to purge the imagery from my memory before coming back to upvote it.

Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now you're getting into the Spooktober spirit, Edna!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw him there, his skin was a dark grey. He died long before I finished. I carefully set him on the table, and did what I had to fulfill my hunger.

Man these might as well be Infant Annihilator lyrics, goddamn. Super gross and violent, I love it. Straight out of a horror movie.

item.title: Author post.
item.content:encoded: So after about HALF A YEAR, I finally got this done. So thanks to gee0765 and TheMightyMcB for crit.
